texto: And like any dog, like any savage, I lay there enjoying myself, harming no man, selling nothing, competing not at all, thinking no evil, smiled on by the sun, bent over by the trees, and softly folded in the arms of the earth.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a John Stewart Collis: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “And like any dog, like any savage, I lay there enjoying myself, harming no man, selling nothing, …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, nature, nature-writing pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de nature-writing.
